root_cause.summary: Prettier's CI on the `renovate/babel` branch (a babel package update) broke 13 jest snapshots across 3 test suites because the updated babel parser reports `import()`-arity errors with new wording. The first failing test, `tests/format/js/_errors_/import/format.test.js > snippet: #0 > format[babel]`, raises `expect(received).rejects.toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot()` and the snapshot diff shows `- Unexpected token (1:8)` (the previous babel error) replaced by `+ \`import()\` requires exactly one or two arguments. (1:1)` (the new babel error). Final totals: 3 failed test suites of 1503, 14 failed tests of 29653, 13 failed snapshots of 12099. The fix is either to re-run jest with `-u` to refresh the snapshots (if the new babel wording is accepted) or to pin babel back. The prettier source is correct — only the embedded babel diagnostic strings drifted.

aliases: + `import()` requires exactly one or two arguments. (1:1)

evidence_spans.reason: First failing snapshot block — `FAIL tests/format/js/_errors_/import/format.test.js`, the failing test variant `snippet: #0 > format[babel]`, the `expect(received).rejects.toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot()` assertion, the snapshot name, the `- Snapshot - 3 / + Received + 3` diff with the actual old-vs-new babel error text, and the stack frame. Sufficient on its own to identify root cause: babel parser-error wording changed.

Final summary block: `13 snapshots failed from 2 test suites` advisory, the Test Suites/Tests/Snapshots totals (3 failed/14 failed/13 failed), and the GHA `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1.` annotation. Confirms the multi_failure scope and the job-level exit.

must_not_claim: the snapshots are correct and the code regressed
import() syntax is invalid in the test source
